author: Dr. Lizard
title: What is the dysplasia threshold required for morphologically-defined MDS in ICC and WHO-5 classifications?
answer: Both ICC and WHO-5 require at least 10% dysplasia in one lineage for morphologically-defined MDS.
detail: WHO-5 allows diagnosis of genetically-defined entities without meeting the 10% dysplasia threshold if defining mutations are present.
Genetically-defined entities in WHO-5 include MDS-SF3B1, MDS-biTP53, and MDS with isolated del(5q).
ICC requires 10% dysplasia for all MDS diagnoses except multi-hit TP53 cases which can be diagnosed without dysplasia.
This represents a key flexibility difference between the two classification systems for genetic entities.
releaseNote: The dysplasia threshold for morphologically-defined MDS varies between ICC and WHO-5 classifications, with ICC requiring 10% dysplasia for all diagnoses except multi-hit TP53 cases.
